title: Genuineness of Writing — Official
text: A signature is presumed to be genuine and authorized if it purports to be the signature, affixed in his official capacity, of: (a) A public employee of the United States. (b) A public employee of any public entity in the United States. (c) A notary public within any state of the United States.

structure.article_title: Presumptions Affecting Acknowledged Writings and Official Writings
